objective: Build a full historical load followed by daily incremental pipeline to extract products, categories, suppliers, and inventory tables from a PostgreSQL operational database to BigQuery for downstream product performance reporting and inventory trend analysis.
business_context: Product catalog and inventory data currently resides in a PostgreSQL operational database and is entirely unavailable for analytics. Surfacing this data in BigQuery will unblock product performance reporting and inventory trend analysis for stakeholder John, enabling data-driven decisions on catalog and stock management.
functional_requirements: Perform a full historical load of all 4 source tables (products, categories, suppliers, inventory) on the first pipeline run
Perform daily incremental sync using updated_at watermark for products and inventory tables
Perform a full daily reload for categories and suppliers as small reference tables
Apply UPSERT logic on primary key in BigQuery to handle late-arriving updates
Airflow DAG must complete before 03:00 AM UTC every day
Load data into BigQuery project verizon-data, dataset verizon_catalog_deah, into tables mirroring source names
Log all pipeline run results to pipeline_audit.pipeline_run_log
Perform COUNT(*) reconciliation between source and target after every run

source_systems: PostgreSQL (host: catalog-db.corp.com, port: 5432, database: product_db, schema: public)
data_requirements.frequency: Full historical load on first run; daily incremental sync thereafter

non_functional.performance: Airflow DAG must complete before 03:00 AM UTC daily
non_functional.scalability: Must handle ~20GB total initial load and sustain ~500MB daily incremental growth
non_functional.latency: Data must be available in BigQuery within 1 hour of source update
non_functional.sla: All 4 tables available in BigQuery dataset verizon_catalog_deah by 03:00 AM UTC daily

constraints.timeline: Full historical load on first run; daily incremental loads must complete by 03:00 AM UTC

assumptions: An updated_at column exists and is reliably populated in the products and inventory tables to support watermark-based incremental extraction
Primary keys are defined, enforced, and unique in all source tables to support UPSERT logic in BigQuery
The pipeline_audit.pipeline_run_log table exists or will be provisioned as part of this engagement
Target BigQuery tables mirror source table names: products, categories, suppliers, inventory within dataset verizon_catalog_deah
inferred_assumptions: Technology environment (dev/staging/prod) is not stated; assumed production given the operational PostgreSQL source and daily SLA requirement
Airflow is assumed to run on GCP Cloud Composer given the BigQuery target on GCP project verizon-data, though no specific Composer version or environment name was provided
acceptance_criteria: Row count in BigQuery equals source row count with 0% tolerance for all 4 tables after every run
Airflow DAG completes before 03:00 AM UTC daily
No duplicate rows on primary key exist in any BigQuery target table
COUNT(*) reconciliation between source and target is executed and validated after every pipeline run
Primary key columns are not null and unique in all BigQuery target tables
All pipeline run results are logged to pipeline_audit.pipeline_run_log
expected_outputs: Airflow DAG orchestrating historical load and daily incremental sync with SLA enforcement
Pipeline run audit records in pipeline_audit.pipeline_run_log after each execution
raw_text:
Ticket: SCRUM-116
Summary: Build PostgreSQL to BigQuery Pipeline for Product Catalog
Status: To Do
Priority: Medium
Assignee: Unassigned
Description:
Project Name: Product Catalog Analytics Platform Requirement Type: New Development Stakeholder: John Objective Product catalog and inventory data sits in a PostgreSQL operational database but is unavailable for analytics. Build a full historical load followed by daily incremental pipeline to extract products, categories, suppliers, and inventory tables to BigQuery for downstream product performance reporting and inventory trend analysis. Expected Outcome: All 4 tables available in BigQuery verizon_catalog_deah dataset by 03:00 AM UTC daily with full history loaded on first run and daily incremental sync using updated_at watermark. Connections and DB Details Source: catalog-db.corp.com , Port: 5432, Database: product_db, Schema: public, Tables: products, categories, suppliers, inventory, Username: sa Target: BigQuery project verizon-data, Dataset: verizon_catalog_deah, Tables: products, categories, suppliers, inventory Functional Requirements Full historical load on first run Daily incremental sync using updated_at watermark for products and inventory Full reload for categories and suppliers (small reference tables, daily) UPSERT logic on primary key to handle late-arriving updates Airflow DAG completing before 03:00 AM UTC Non-Functional Requirements Latency: Data available in BigQuery within 1 hour of source update Data volume: ~20GB total, ~500MB daily incremental Acceptance Criteria Success Conditions: Row count in BigQuery equals source with 0% tolerance Airflow DAG completes before 03:00 AM UTC daily No duplicate rows on primary key in BigQuery Validation Rules: COUNT(*) reconciliation between source and target after every run Primary key must be not null and unique Results logged to pipeline_audit.pipeline_run_log
